items.prompt: Which statement best defines data annotation in this chapter?
choices: Exporting raw sensor logs without metadata
Attaching task-defined labels, tags, or structured metadata to raw records for supervised learning
Training a model without any human review
Removing duplicate rows from a warehouse table
items.explain: Annotation pairs inputs with intended outputs so supervised models can learn mappings.

items.prompt: Why does the chapter emphasize label quality early?
choices: Label quality often bounds achievable accuracy more tightly than model architecture alone
Quality matters only for tabular data
items.explain: Especially in high-stakes domains, labeling errors propagate into deployment behavior.

items.prompt: In Example 4.3, person and location spans receive distinct entity types under a standard NER schema.
items.explain: Named-entity recognition assigns types such as person and location to identified spans.

items.prompt: Name two non-text modalities previewed in this clip and one label type for each.
items.rubric: Any two of image/audio/video with a matching label type (e.g., boxes on images, transcripts on audio, frame tracks on video).
items.sample_answer: Image: bounding boxes or segmentation masks; audio: transcripts or emotion tags; video: per-frame object labels or activity tags.

items.prompt: In Example 4.2, how are the two customer feedback sentences labeled?
items.explain: The excellent-service sentence is positive; the disappointing-product sentence is negative.
